title: NOC 10021 – Banking, Credit, and Other Investment Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Banking, Credit, and Other Investment Managers classified under NOC 10021 fall within one of the most senior and in-demand occupational groups in Canada’s immigration system. These professionals oversee core banking operations, credit administration, lending portfolios, and investment activities—making them highly attractive candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).
common_job_titles: Assistant operations manager – banking, credit and investment
Corporate services manager – banking, credit and investment
Personal services manager – banking, credit and investment
duties: Professionals in this group may perform some or all of the following:
Direct branch or departmental operations in banks, trust companies, or credit unions
Ensure compliance with internal policies and regulatory frameworks
Promote lending, investment, and banking services to attract new clients
Assess customer needs and respond to financial enquiries
Approve or decline loan and credit applications within authority limits
Monitor processing of loan files and credit investigations
Review monthly financial and performance reports
Manage corporate, commercial, and personal credit portfolios
Approve or recommend credit limits and repayment schedules
Ensure credit policies follow legislation and guidelines
employment_requirements: Occupations in TEER 0 usually require a university degree or college diploma and several years of experience in a related field.
job_outlook: Between 2024 and 2033, Canada is projected to create 31,200 new positions for Banking, Credit, and Other Investment Managers, while approximately 34,400 job seekers may enter the field. For internationally trained professionals—particularly applicants from India—this represents encouraging opportunities when supported by a targeted immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Banking, Credit, and Other Investment Managers remain in strong demand across Canada’s financial-services sector, including banking, credit unions, mortgage lenders, insurance firms, and investment institutions. Hiring activity expanded significantly after the pandemic and is projected to remain steady in the coming years.
Excellent immigration opportunities for experienced overseas professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $82,500 to $165,348, depending on experience, location, and specialization.
Why Banking & Investment Managers Choose Canada
Professionals immigrating under NOC 10021 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
What Is NOC 10021 (Banking, Credit, and Other Investment Managers)?

benefits: In addition to salaries, many employers offer:
Employer contributions to national pension and employment-insurance programs
